questions: What do you mean by 'secular ones fail'?
How does 'Look at Communism and Marxism which is militantly anti-religion.' support 'secular ones fail'?
Can you explain why you assume 'Communism and Marxism' are relevant to 'secular ones fail'?
What do you mean by 'men calling women bitch has come with beatings in the past.'?
How does 'White people calling black people slurs have come with beatings and deaths in the past.' contribute to the main claim?
Can you provide more details about 'beatings and deaths in the past'?
Can you provide further justification for why people's obsession with state rights is one of the few things holding us back from abolishing the EC?
What do you mean by 'people's obsession with state rights'? How does that relate to the EC?
Is there any evidence to support the claim that people's obsession with state rights is one of the few things holding us back from abolishing the EC?
What do you mean by 'people protesting now shows nothing about what Trump or Obama has done as president'?
How do you know that people protesting now doesn't tell us something about what Trump or Obama has done as president?
Is there any evidence that supports the idea that people protesting now tells us something about what Trump or Obama has done as president?
What do you mean by 'radical leftist'?
How do you know that Jesus' teachings were ignored by conservatives?
Can you explain why you think there would be a correlation between Jesus being a radical leftist and more inclusive communities?
What do you mean by 'people'?
How do you know they want us to stop vetting?
Can you provide some examples of why people might want us to stop vetting?
What do you mean by 'can't'?
How do you know that?
Is there any evidence that would convince you that this isn't true?
What do you mean by 'better'?
How does 'giving it to someone who can handle it better' help improve government?
Can you provide some examples of what you mean by 'someone who can handle it better'?
What evidence do you have to support that the stock market performance is indeed due to a transfer of wealth from public to private hands?
Can you consider other reasons why the stock market might perform well during this time period?
How do you know that the government spending was the cause of the market takeoff rather than another factor?
What do you mean by 'everyone'?
How did you find out that calling someone a transsexual was offensive?
Are there any other reasons why people might have thought less of you after you called someone a transsexual?
What do you mean by 'alt-right' is an insult?
How did you determine that 'alt-right' is an insult?
Are there any other reasons you think someone would fail to have a substantive dialogue with you?
What do you mean by 'human history'?
Can you explain how studying human history led you to conclude that you haven't been swayed?
How do you account for the possibility that you may have missed something important while studying human history?
What do you mean by 'average weight of a US male'?
How do you know that the average weight of male police officers is likely also 195?
Don't you think it's unfair to assume that just because the average weight of a US male is 195, the same is true for male police officers?
What do you mean by 'other factors'?
How do you know those other factors contribute to increased deaths in car crashes?
Can you explain why you assume other factors exist without providing evidence?
What do you mean by "intersex"? How does this relate to gender?
Why did you say "I identify with four of the sexes" instead of just saying "I identify as a woman"?
Can you explain why you think people might criticize intersex individuals?
What evidence do you have to support the claim that repeating something doesn't actually count as proof?
How might someone who disagrees with this claim respond?
Can you think of another way to justify this claim?
What evidence do you have to support the claim that most altcoins are not cryptocurrencies?
How do you know that people only buy alt coins for financial gain?
Can you explain how you came to the conclusion that there are other factors beyond financial gain that lead someone to create an alt coin?
What do you mean by 'abnormal development'?
How did the doctors determine that you were developing abnormally?
Can you think of another reason why the doctors might have recommended abortion?
What evidence do you have to support the assumption that the dominant culture aids the idea that Native Americans can't remove or harm mainstream American culture?
How do you know that Native Americans don't want to erase or damage mainstream culture?
Can you think of any other reasons why Native Americans mightn't try to erase or damage mainstream culture?
What do you mean by 'context'?
How do you know that context is always necessary to understand someone's intentions?
Can you provide examples where someone's intentions were misinterpreted due to lack of context?
What do you mean by 'spread a lot of knowledge'?
How do you know British colonialism spread a lot of knowledge?
Don't you think British colonialism might have had some negative consequences too?
What do you mean by 'living life by the edge'?
How do you know that tattoos are commonly associated with impulsiveness, self-indulgence, and living life by the edge?
Are there any exceptions to the rule that parents should forbid their kids from getting tattoos?
What do you mean by 'rational skeptic point of view'?
How does 'extremely improbable' relate to probability?
Can you explain why you assume they don't have any specialized knowledge?
Probe the assumption that you could fight off an attack dog. What do you mean by 'fairly certain'?
Challenge the claim that you've seen attack dog training videos. Can you provide more context or details?
Ask yourself whether there may be any inconsistencies between your personal experience and the claim you make. How might someone who hasn't seen those videos interpret your statement?
What do you mean by 'I never said or expected everyone to be friends'?
How do you know that you never said or expected everyone to be friends?
Can you explain why you didn't expect everyone to be friends?
What do you mean by 'I don't think I used the right phrasing'?
Can you provide more justification for why you consider this argument to be moral?
How do you know that you didn't use the right phrasing?
What do you mean by 'stopped'?
Why do you think this example refutes your statement?
How can you assume that someone will stop someone else with a pen?
What do you mean by 'you can't make that statement and then proceed to tell me to prove you wrong'?
How do you know that 50% of cops are bad?
Don't you think it would be difficult to determine whether someone is a good cop or a bad cop simply by looking at them?
What do you mean by 'fundamentally bad'?
Can you provide some examples of how this might play out in practice?
Don't states often rely on surveillance to maintain public safety?
What do you mean by 'assumptions'?
How did you determine that those assumptions are, at best guesses?
Can you provide more information about 'things can EASILY turn out differently'?
What do you mean by 'American'?
How does someone's ethnicity affect their nationality?
Can you provide more examples of how someone might identify themselves?
Ask the writer why they say 'In case you didn't know.'?
What evidence do you have to support the claim that capital gains should be taxed the same as ordinary income?
Are there any unstated assumptions behind the belief that capital gains should be taxed the same as ordinary income?
How does the author know that there have been debates over the tax code overhaul?
What do you mean by 'race'?
How does 'manned moon landing' serve as a good example of a finish line?
Are there any other mile markers besides the finish line that would indicate success in the space race?
What do you mean by 'it is better'?
How do you know that people getting bullied because of what they wear or if they are poor is such a big thing?
Are there any other reasons why it might be better that school uniforms exist?
What evidence do you have to support that 'that's not me'?
How does 'it is repeated or foregrounded through emphasis and evidence selection.' support 'that's not me'?
Why do you think someone would say 'that's not me', rather than simply saying 'I don't agree'?
What do you mean by 'illegal'?
How do you know that people won't think you're a racist?
Are there any other reasons why someone might use 'illegal' as a slur?
Can you provide additional reasons why you think you might have an intellectual disorder?
What do you mean by 'activists are annoying'?
How do you know that activism leads to discomfort?
Are there any other reasons why activists might be seen as annoying?
What do you mean by 'actual censorship'?
How do you know that someone who disagrees isn't just trying to provoke a response?
Can you provide more context about why you think this is a slippery slope fallacy?
What do you mean by 'the power of the judiciary'?
Can you provide more evidence to support this claim?
How do you justify that 'the power of the judiciary is not being weakened at all' given that there have been reports of Trump wielding power over the judiciary?
What do you mean by 'I was honestly starting to feel attacked'?
How did you come to the conclusion that you were 'starting to feel attacked'?
Can you provide more details regarding 'would have gotten super pissed'?
What do you mean by 'it's not a human inside the mother'?
How do you know that society may improve morally?
Why do you assume that some people may feel uncomfortable with the idea of abortion being legal?
What do you mean by 'the versions of the drug are the same for both animals and humans'?
How do you know the versions of the drug are the same for both animals and humans?
Don't you think there might be some difference between the versions of the drug for animals and humans?
What do you mean by 'worship'?
How does 'There is a difference between worshiping a god worshiping nature.' imply 'I also think there's a difference between religion spirituality.'?
Can you provide more details about 'other factors'?
What do you mean by 'judge'?
How do you know this is true for everyone?
Are there any other reasons why someone might think they can judge another person without thinking about it?
What do you mean by "comparisons"?
How do you know that there's just a minor difference?
Can you provide some examples of those comparisons?
What do you mean by 'false or harmful beliefs'?
How do you know that people arriving at false or harmful beliefs is more likely than people arriving at true or beneficial beliefs?
Can you provide some examples of how people might think for themselves and still arrive at true or beneficial beliefs?
Can you provide additional evidence to support this claim?
How do you define 'wet'?
What would happen if you assumed the opposite of this claim?
What do you mean by "transphobic"?
How can you justify that someone who identifies as a trans woman isn't actually a woman?
Can you explain why you think someone's attempt at being respectful undermines their core point?
What do you mean by 'old people'?
How did you determine that they only ask old people the questions?
Is there any evidence to support the claim that they only ask old people the questions?
What do you mean by 'voting in elections'?
How does 'you are able to vote in elections and help make a difference for who you want to represent you.' logically follow from 'you are able to vote in elections'?
Are there any situations where someone wouldn't have this right?
What do you mean by 'Al Gore isn't and never was a climate scientist'?
How do you know Al Gore isn't and never was a climate scientist?
Can you provide some examples of how actual professionals have made less sensationalist, more evidence based predictions about climate change?
What do you mean by 'one could still have dysphoria'?
How do you know that gender is a social construct?
Are there any other ways to define gender?
What evidence do you have to support this claim?
How do you know that working in the industry makes you have moral qualms?
Can you think of another explanation why someone might have moral qualms about working in the industry after maturing?
What do you mean by 'judge things on theory'?
How do you know that producing evidence and studies is impossible?
Don't you think it's possible to study two different countries without destroying them?
What do you mean by 'ideal world', and how does this relate to the real world?
How do you know that the hypothetical mother would have to pay in an ideal world?
Don't you think it's unfair to assume that the hypothetical mother would have to pay without considering other factors?
What do you mean by 'long run'?
How does 'putting the responsibility on the victims' lead to more problems?
Are there any other ways to approach this problem?
